untranslatable_reason: Signal requires identification of swing extremes, measured move objectives (MMO), pullback depth thresholds, and countertrend thrust magnitude — none of which can be computed from single-bar indicators. The core logic demands multi-bar pattern recognition (swing A confirmation, pullback failure relative to trend extreme, MMO proximity) and parameterized thresholds ([THRESHOLD_NEEDED] values) that are not present. No combination of allowed indicators can reliably detect 'pullback fails to penetrate previous trend extreme' or 'price exhausts near MMO' without explicit swing/pattern detection.
translation_notes: This is a swing-pattern reversion signal that requires structural pattern recognition (identifying swings, measuring moves, testing extremes) rather than oscillator or trend-following indicators. The source text mentions 'lack of conviction beyond previous trend extreme' and 'equilibrium,' but operationalizing this requires candlestick pattern logic or swing-high/swing-low tracking, which are not available in the allowed indicator set. A backtest engine would need custom pattern detection functions to translate this rule.
